title: Partner/Spouse Visa and Residence Permit - Netherlands
title_local: Machtiging tot voorlopig verblijf (MVV) en verblijfsvergunning voor partner
summary: Non-EU/EEA nationals who want to join their partner in the Netherlands usually need an MVV entry visa followed by a residence permit for family reunification.
steps: Sponsor in the Netherlands submits an application to IND on behalf of the foreign partner.
IND assesses whether the sponsor meets the income requirement and relationship requirements.
The foreign partner attends a biometrics appointment at the Dutch embassy or consulate.
If approved, the foreign partner receives a long-stay visa (MVV) valid for 90 days.
Within 3 days of arrival in the Netherlands, register at your municipality (Gemeente).
The IND will issue the residence permit (verblijfsvergunning) with family reunification basis.
Attend the IND biometrics appointment in the Netherlands to collect the residence card.
If required, complete civic integration obligations within the legal period.
documents: Valid passport of the foreign partner
Marriage certificate or proof of registered partnership (apostilled and translated where required)
Proof of sponsor's income (employer statement and payslips)
Proof of sponsor's residence permit or Dutch/EU citizenship
Proof of accommodation in the Netherlands
office: IND (Immigratie en Naturalisatiedienst) and Dutch embassy/consulate; municipality (Gemeente) after arrival.
fee: EUR210 for MVV and residence permit (combined application fee, 2024 reference)
notes: Some nationalities require MVV while others are exempt; verify by nationality before applying.
